title: Software Engineering
description: Study of software engineering principles, methodologies, and practices for developing large-scale software systems. Covers requirements engineering, software architecture and design patterns, agile development (Scrum, Kanban), version control with Git, continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD), testing strategies (unit, integration, system, acceptance), code quality metrics, and project management. Students work in teams on a semester-long software project following agile methodology with regular sprint reviews. The course emphasizes practical skills including code review, refactoring, and technical documentation. Prerequisite: CS201 ensures students have sufficient programming maturity for team-based development.
topics: Design patterns: observer, factory, strategy, MVC
Version control with Git and GitHub
outcomes: Apply agile development methodology to team projects
Design software architectures using appropriate patterns
Write requirements specifications and user stories
Use version control and CI/CD tools professionally
Manage software projects with proper estimation and tracking
